science.title: Four pillars, one protocol
science.subtitle: We work on four mechanisms that compound: cell signalling, dermal scaffolding, mitochondrial energy and controlled delivery. The product line is built around that order.
science.leadParagraph: Mitoderm is not a single hero molecule with a brand name on top. It's a layered protocol — exosomes do the signalling, polynucleotides rebuild the scaffold, NAD+ powers the recovery, and microneedling delivery makes sure all three land where they're supposed to land. Each pillar has a home in the product line.

exosomes.title: Exosomes — the signalling layer
exosomes.lead: Nanometre-scale extracellular vesicles that carry a multi-protein, multi-RNA payload between cells.
exosomes.body: Free growth factors hit a barrier and stop. Exosomes survive the trip, deliver several signals at once and participate in a richer conversation with the recipient cells. The V-Tech serum leads with carrier design so the payload reaches the upper dermis intact.

polynucleotides.title: Polynucleotides — rebuilding the scaffold
polynucleotides.lead: Long nucleotide chains that bind water and instruct fibroblasts to rebuild dermal scaffolding.
polynucleotides.body: The V-Tech Gel Mask layers low-molecular polynucleotides together with biomimetic peptides and snail mucin. Inside a session, the mask is the seal that turns serum delivery into structural change — density, plumpness and a quieted post-procedure barrier.

nad.title: NAD+ — powering the recovery
nad.lead: The coenzyme central to mitochondrial energy production and DNA repair — the substrate of visible glow.
nad.body: EXO-NAD is a three-step longevity peeling: pH normalizer, peptide-priming Longevity Serum and the NAD+ peel itself. Run on alternating weeks alongside V-Tech to address signalling and substrate without colliding the two protocols.

delivery.title: Microneedling delivery — the protocol's exit step
delivery.lead: All three pillars are only as good as the channels they travel through.
delivery.body: MitoPen E30-Ultima is the delivery instrument the V-Tech and Exosignal protocols are written for: five speeds, dial-locked depth, sterile click-in cartridges. Mitoscan completes the loop on the diagnostic side — chair-side HD imaging at baseline, mid-course and end-of-course.

protocol.title: The four pillars in one workflow
protocol.text: Diagnose with Mitoscan. Plan a course around the indication. Deliver via MitoPen at the locked depth. Follow up with a freeze-frame at the next visit. The same four mechanisms, every time.

cta.title: Bring the protocol into your clinic
cta.text: Pricing, training and protocol support — we work with your existing menu rather than against it.

concerns.title: Start with the patient's complaint
concerns.subtitle: Four entry points that match how a consultation actually opens — pick the concern, land on the line and protocol that addresses it.

density.title: Loss of density & structural aging
density.text: V-Tech serum, gel mask and Exotech — the exosome face system for density, plumpness and post-procedure recovery.
density.explainer: From the mid-20s collagen synthesis falls roughly 1% a year, and the fat pads and bone that scaffold the face slowly recede. The result reads as flatness and slackening rather than lines — which is why a density protocol works on the structure underneath, not only the surface.

hair.title: Hair loss & scalp thinning
hair.text: ExoSignal serum and spray — protocol pairing for follicle health and visible scalp coverage.
hair.explainer: Thinning starts at the follicle, not the strand: shortening growth cycles and a miniaturising follicle produce finer hairs and a wider parting long before any bald patch. Acting early — while the follicle is still active — is what keeps a protocol effective.

longevity.title: Dullness, fatigue, longevity
longevity.text: EXO-NAD three-step peeling — mitochondrial recovery and visible glow without resurfacing downtime.
longevity.explainer: Dull, tired skin is often a cellular-energy story: as mitochondrial output and NAD+ levels fall, cells repair and renew more slowly and the surface loses its bounce and glow. A longevity protocol works on that energy layer rather than resurfacing the skin.

devices.title: Diagnose, deliver, document
devices.text: Mitoscan for HD scalp and follicle imaging; MitoPen E30-Ultima for motorised auto-stamp delivery.
devices.explainer: You can't improve what you don't measure. HD imaging at day 0 sets the baseline every later result is judged against, and controlled, repeatable delivery is what turns a good serum into a reproducible outcome across patients.

intro.title: Exoxe Exosome in Israel
intro.text: MitoDerm combines advanced aesthetic technology with science to bring innovative solutions to the field of aesthetics. Are you a doctor or cosmetologist looking to enhance your services? Let's innovate together!

about.title: Science is our craft. Beauty is the outcome.
about.tagline: Mitoderm brings clinical-grade exosome technology to aesthetic practitioners — from Israel to the world.
about.missionTitle: Built for the clinic, grounded in science
about.missionText: We partner with researchers, formulators and clinicians to bring exosome-based treatments to professional practices — products, protocols and training designed around real clinical outcomes, not trends.
about.scienceText: Exosomes are tiny vesicles loaded with growth factors, peptides and signaling molecules. Applied professionally, they instruct skin cells to repair, regenerate and produce more collagen — accelerating recovery, refining texture and visibly restoring radiance.
about.founderText: Ilona founded Mitoderm to bring clinical-grade exosome technology to aesthetic practitioners across Israel — pairing hands-on professional training with a protocol-led product line built on science.
about.ctaText: Get pricing, protocols and onboarding for your clinic.

item1.title: What are exosomes?
item1.text: Exosomes are tiny vesicles, rich in growth factors, proteins, and regenerative signals. These powerful messengers interact with your body's cells, stimulating healing and rejuvenation.

item2.title: How do exosomes work?
item2.text: Exosomes deliver growth factors, proteins, and regenerative signals directly to your body's target cells. When applied to a treatment area, they communicate with these cells to promote repair, regeneration, and rejuvenation. By harnessing your body's natural healing processes, exosome therapy stimulates tissue renewal, boosts collagen production, and enhances overall cellular health. The result is significant improvement in the treated area—whether it's skin, hair or other specific regions.

item3.title: What conditions are helped by exosomes?
item3.text: At Mitoderm, our exosome therapy offers transformative solutions—including skin rejuvenation to enhance texture and reduce signs of aging; hair restoration to promote growth and address thinning hair; reduction of acne scars for smoother skin and post-procedure healing to accelerate recovery—all by unlocking your body's natural ability to renew.

item1.name: Victoria Aslanyan
item1.text: As a doctor and clinic owner, I integrate advanced exosome treatments into my practice. The results are outstanding! My patients see smoother, rejuvenated skin, and the products have become a cornerstone of my clinic's offerings. I'm definitely continuing with these treatments!"

item2.name: Gregory Dyachenko
item2.text: I had an amazing experience with the exosome treatment for hair thinning, and I want to thank Ilona especially. In just a few months, my hair has gone from thinning to fuller and healthier. I'm truly impressed with the transformation.

privacy.title: Privacy notice
privacy.intro: This notice explains what personal data Mitoderm collects when you visit the Site (exoskin.co.il) or contact us, why we collect it, how we protect and share it, and the rights you have under Israeli law. It is written to align with Israel's Protection of Privacy Law, 5741-1981 as amended by Amendment 13 (in force August 2025), and — for visitors in the EU/EEA — with the GDPR.

sections.heading: Who we are — the database holder
paragraphs: The Site is operated by Mitoderm Academy, of Rafael Eitan 38, Ramat Gan 5590500, Israel ("Mitoderm", "we", "us").

For the purposes of the Protection of Privacy Law, Mitoderm is the Owner and the Manager of the customer-enquiries database that this Site feeds. Our hosting and email providers act as Holders / processors on our behalf. Questions about the database may be directed to our privacy contact below.

sections.heading: What we collect
paragraphs: We collect the minimum necessary to answer your enquiries and keep the Site functional. We do not collect special-category data and ask that you not send clinical or patient information through the Site.
bullets: Contact-form data: name, email, phone (optional), clinic name (optional) and the message you write.
Clinic-application data: the above plus city, professional licence number and an optional referral code.
Waitlist sign-ups for upcoming product lines: email address.
Marketing-attribution data, only when present: UTM parameters and the page you arrived on.
Technical logs created by our hosting provider — IP address, user agent, request path — retained briefly for security and abuse prevention.
Cookies and analytics, only if you opt in at the banner: a consent cookie plus, on consent, Google Analytics 4. We never sell your data.

sections.heading: Why we collect it (purposes)
paragraphs: Each category is used only for the purpose it was given for:
bullets: To answer your enquiry and provide the information you requested.
To onboard your clinic and manage our commercial relationship.
To send product, training and protocol information you have asked for.
To send marketing communications — only where you have given separate, specific consent (see below).
To secure the Site, prevent abuse, and meet our legal and accounting obligations.
To understand, in aggregate, which content is useful — analytics only, and only after consent.

sections.heading: Legal basis
paragraphs: We process your contact data on the basis of your consent — you chose to send the form. Security logging rests on our legitimate interest in protecting the Site. Analytics and any marketing run only on the separate consent you give. You can withdraw any consent at any time, without affecting processing already carried out.

sections.heading: Marketing communications
paragraphs: We send marketing messages (email, SMS or WhatsApp) only where you have actively opted in, in line with section 30A of the Communications Law (Telecommunications and Broadcasting), 5742-1982. Every marketing message carries a one-click way to unsubscribe, and you can also opt out at any time by writing to the contact below. Operational replies to your own enquiry are not marketing and are sent regardless.

sections.heading: Who we share it with
paragraphs: We do not sell personal data. We disclose the minimum necessary to:
bullets: Our service providers acting as processors under contract — website hosting (Vercel Inc.) and transactional email (Resend, Inc.).
Our partner clinics — only where you ask us to route your enquiry to a specific clinic.
Authorities or advisers where we are legally required to, or to establish or defend legal claims.

sections.heading: International transfers
paragraphs: Some of our processors (Vercel, Resend) store data on servers located outside Israel, including in the United States. We transfer data abroad only in accordance with the Protection of Privacy (Transfer of Data to Databases Abroad) Regulations, 5761-2001 and, for EU/EEA data, on the basis of appropriate safeguards. Recipients are bound by contract to protect the data to a standard comparable to Israeli law and not to transfer it onward without a lawful basis.

sections.heading: How long we keep it (retention)
paragraphs: We keep data only as long as the purpose requires:
bullets: Enquiry / lead records: up to 24 months after the last contact, then deleted or anonymised.
Clinic-partner accounts: for the life of the relationship plus the period required by accounting and tax law (currently 7 years).
Waitlist emails: until the line launches plus 12 months, then deleted.
Security logs: rolled off within 30 days.

sections.heading: How we protect it
paragraphs: We apply technical and organisational measures appropriate to the sensitivity of the data: encryption in transit (HTTPS/HSTS), strict access controls, rate limiting, signed administrative sessions and audit logging. No system is perfectly secure, but we work to industry-standard safeguards.

sections.heading: Data-breach notification
paragraphs: In line with Amendment 13 to the Protection of Privacy Law, if a serious security incident affecting your personal data occurs, we will notify the Israeli Privacy Protection Authority and, where required, the affected individuals, within the statutory timeframes.

sections.heading: Your rights
paragraphs: Under Israeli law (and the GDPR where it applies) you may:
bullets: Access the data we hold about you (s.13 of the Law).
Have inaccurate data corrected or completed (s.14).
Request deletion of your data, subject to our legal retention duties.
Object to direct-marketing use of your data at any time.
Withdraw consent, and — for EU/EEA visitors — request portability or restriction.
Lodge a complaint with the Privacy Protection Authority (see below).

sections.heading: Cookies
paragraphs: We use three cookie categories — strictly necessary, analytics and marketing. Only strictly-necessary cookies run without your consent. You control the rest at the banner, and can change your choice at any time. Full details are on our Cookies page.

sections.heading: Children
paragraphs: The Site addresses healthcare professionals and is not directed at children. We do not knowingly collect personal data from anyone under the age of 16. If you believe a minor has provided us data, contact us and we will delete it.

sections.heading: Supervisory authority
paragraphs: You may contact or complain to the Israeli Privacy Protection Authority ("the Authority") — https://www.gov.il/en/departments/the_privacy_protection_authority. EU/EEA visitors may also contact their local data-protection authority.

sections.heading: Changes & governing language
paragraphs: We may update this notice; material changes are reflected in the "Last updated" date above. This notice is published in English, Russian and Hebrew. In the event of any discrepancy, the Hebrew version prevails.

privacy.contactText: Privacy questions, requests or to exercise a right: write to info@mitoderm.com. We will respond within 30 days.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.intro: These terms govern your use of the Site (exoskin.co.il), operated by Mitoderm Academy of Rafael Eitan 38, Ramat Gan 5590500, Israel ("Mitoderm"). By using the Site you accept these terms. If you do not agree, please do not use the Site.

sections.heading: Who we are
paragraphs: The Site is owned and operated by Mitoderm Academy, Rafael Eitan 38, Ramat Gan 5590500, Israel. You can reach us at the contact address at the foot of these terms.

sections.heading: Eligibility
paragraphs: The Site presents professional aesthetic-medicine products intended for use by licensed clinicians. Information about specific products, protocols and devices is addressed to professionals; it is not medical advice for the general public.

sections.heading: No online sales
paragraphs: The Site is informational. It does not sell products or take payment online. Orders, pricing and supply are arranged separately and offline (for example via WhatsApp or direct contact), under terms agreed at that time. Accordingly, the distance-selling provisions of the Consumer Protection Law do not apply to use of the Site itself.

sections.heading: Acceptable use
paragraphs: You agree not to misuse the Site or attempt to disrupt it.
bullets: Do not attempt to gain unauthorised access to any non-public area, including admin endpoints.
Do not scrape the Site for commercial competitive purposes.
Do not submit content that infringes others' rights or violates law.

sections.heading: Submissions
paragraphs: When you send us a message or application through the Site, you confirm the information is accurate and that you are entitled to share it. You grant Mitoderm a limited licence to store and use that submission for the purpose of responding to you and managing the relationship, in accordance with our Privacy notice.

sections.heading: Intellectual property
paragraphs: All text, imagery, design, source code and trademarks on the Site are owned by Mitoderm or its licensors. You may share links and short quotations for editorial purposes with attribution; broader reuse requires our written permission.

sections.heading: No medical advice; no guaranteed outcome
paragraphs: Content on the Site is informational and reflects clinical experience and product-level documentation. It is not a substitute for an individualised consultation with a qualified practitioner, and nothing on the Site is medical advice to the public. Treatment outcomes are individual and are not guaranteed. Patients should consult a licensed clinician before any procedure.

sections.heading: Third-party services
paragraphs: Forms route through Resend for email delivery; the Site is hosted on Vercel; optional analytics is Google Analytics 4 (loaded only after consent). Each provider has its own terms; we select reputable processors with appropriate safeguards.

sections.heading: Limitation of liability
paragraphs: The Site is provided on an "as is" and "as available" basis. To the maximum extent permitted by law, Mitoderm is not liable for indirect, incidental or consequential damages arising from use of the Site or reliance on its content. Where liability cannot be excluded, our total aggregate liability is limited to ILS 5,000. Nothing in these terms limits liability for gross negligence, wilful misconduct, or any liability that cannot be limited under mandatory law.

sections.heading: Force majeure
paragraphs: We are not responsible for any failure or delay caused by events beyond our reasonable control, including outages of hosting or communications providers, security incidents, strikes, or acts of government or nature.

sections.heading: Severability
paragraphs: If any provision of these terms is held invalid or unenforceable, the remaining provisions continue in full force, and the invalid provision is replaced by a valid one that most closely reflects its intent.

sections.heading: Changes
paragraphs: We may update these terms. Material changes will be reflected in the "Last updated" date above and, where significant, flagged on the Site for a reasonable period. Continued use after a change takes effect implies acceptance of the revised terms.

sections.heading: Governing law & language
paragraphs: These terms are governed by the laws of the State of Israel, and disputes are subject to the exclusive jurisdiction of the competent courts of the Tel Aviv-Yafo district. These terms are published in English, Russian and Hebrew; in the event of any discrepancy, the Hebrew version prevails.

terms.contactText: Questions about these terms: write to info@mitoderm.com.

cookies.title: Cookie policy
cookies.intro: This page explains the cookies and similar local-storage technologies the Site uses, what each is for, and how to control them. Only strictly-necessary technologies run before you choose at the cookie banner; analytics and marketing run only with your consent.

sections.heading: Your choice
paragraphs: When you first visit, a banner lets you accept or decline non-essential cookies. We remember that choice and do not load analytics or marketing technologies unless you accept. You can change your mind at any time by clearing the consent value in your browser — the banner will appear again.

sections.heading: Categories
paragraphs: Strictly necessary — required for the Site to function and to remember your consent; always on. Analytics — aggregate, non-identifying usage measurement; only on consent. Marketing — used to measure and target advertising; only on consent, and only if such tools are configured.

glossary.exosomes: Nano-sized vesicles (~30–150 nm) released by cells, carrying growth factors, peptides and signalling molecules. In aesthetics they instruct skin cells to repair, regenerate and produce more collagen.
glossary.niacinamide: Vitamin B3. Reinforces the skin barrier, evens tone, reduces sebum production and redness. Tolerated by most skin types.
glossary.hyaluronic acid: A glycosaminoglycan that holds up to 1000× its weight in water. Plumps fine lines, restores bounce, and helps active ingredients spread evenly.
glossary.peptide: Short chain of amino acids that signal skin cells. Different peptides stimulate collagen, elasticity, or barrier repair.
glossary.collagen: Structural protein providing skin firmness. Production drops ~1% per year after age 25; many actives target its synthesis.
glossary.nad+: Coenzyme central to cellular energy. Topical NAD+ supports mitochondrial recovery — relevant for longevity protocols.
glossary.retinol: Vitamin A derivative. Accelerates cell turnover, smooths texture, reduces fine lines. Photosensitising — use in evening protocols.
glossary.ahas: Alpha-hydroxy acids (glycolic, lactic, mandelic). Water-soluble exfoliants targeting tone and texture.
glossary.bhas: Beta-hydroxy acids (salicylic). Oil-soluble — penetrate sebum to clear pores. Best for acne-prone skin.
glossary.polynucleotide: Long chains of nucleotides that bind water and signal fibroblasts to rebuild dermal scaffolding. Particularly relevant for post-procedure barrier soothing and density.
glossary.snail mucin: Filtered secretion rich in glycoproteins, hyaluronic acid and copper peptides. Calms, hydrates and supports barrier recovery.
glossary.biomimetic peptide: Lab-designed peptide that mimics a signal the skin already understands — instructs fibroblasts to repair, restructure or quiet inflammation.
glossary.microneedling: Controlled micro-channels created with a motorised needle pen. Triggers a wound-healing cascade and, more importantly, delivers actives past the stratum corneum.
glossary.lyophilised: Freeze-dried — water removed at low temperature to keep delicate cargo stable until activation at the chair.

linePage.synergyBody: When combining synthetic exosomes with equipment, the synergistic effect makes the result much stronger. Exosomes don't just "add" but "triple" the result! After a controlled injury, the material is dripped onto the skin, effectively giving the skin a reservoir of growth factors, peptides, proteins, and plant stem cells, so the skin uses new reserves and rebuilds itself.
